concept.title: DITA Project Files
concept.shortdesc: Project files define reusable, shareable build configurations that replace long command-line arguments with declarative XML — enabling consistent, repeatable builds across teams and CI systems.

section.title: Why project files?
section.p: Without project files, every build requires a long command line:
This is error-prone, hard to share, and impossible to version-control meaningfully. Project files solve all three problems by capturing the entire build definition in XML.

section.title: Project file anatomy
section.p: A DITA-OT project file uses the https://www.dita-ot.org/project namespace and contains three core elements:

dlentry.dd: Names the input map and any shared settings. Multiple deliverables can reference the same context, ensuring consistency.
Defines one output — its transtype, output directory, and parameters. A project can contain many deliverables.
Nested inside a deliverable — specifies the transtype and its parameter overrides.

section.title: Minimal example
section.p: A project file that builds both HTML5 and PDF from the same map:
section.codeblock:
<project xmlns="https://www.dita-ot.org/project">
<context name="training-guide" id="ctx-training"> <input href="extense-supermap.ditamap"/> </context>
<deliverable name="Web Site"> <context idref="ctx-training"/> <output href="./output/html5"/> <publication transtype="html5"> <param name="args.css" value="brand.css"/> <param name="args.cssroot" value="css"/> <param name="nav-toc" value="full"/> </publication> </deliverable>
<deliverable name="Print Manual"> <context idref="ctx-training"/> <output href="./output/pdf"/> <publication transtype="pdf"> <param name="args.chapter.layout" value="MINITOC"/> <param name="args.bookmap-order" value="retain"/> </publication> </deliverable>
</project>
section.p: DITA-OT builds every deliverable in sequence, reusing the shared context.

section.title: Advanced patterns
section.p: Use DITAVAL profiles inside a context to produce filtered variants:
Define separate contexts for different maps and build them all together:
section.codeblock: <context name="user-guide" id="ctx-user"> <input href="maps/user-guide.ditamap"/> </context> <context name="api-reference" id="ctx-api"> <input href="maps/api-ref.ditamap"/> </context>
section.p: Project files can include other project files for modular build definitions:

section.title: Properties files vs. project files
section.p: DITA-OT also supports .properties files — simple key=value pairs loaded with --propertyfile. Choose the right tool:
Use properties files for quick, single-transtype builds. Use project files when you manage multiple deliverables, filtered variants, or CI pipelines.

ul.li: Version-control your project files alongside your DITA source — they are part of your publication definition.
Name deliverables clearly — name="Admin PDF (v3.2)" makes build logs easy to scan.
Use relative paths for input maps and output directories so the project works across machines.
Keep one project file per product — include shared definitions from a common file to avoid duplication.
Test locally before CI — run dita --project=build.xml on your machine to catch configuration errors early.
